meta.title: DeadSignal
meta.description: DeadSignal is a fixed narrative investigation interface for exploring The Helix Incident.

objectives.title: Current Objectives

traceBlackout.meta: Relay report and encrypted fragment are now connected

objectives.guidance: Complete objectives by reading clues, unlocking files, and creating valid deductions.

serverRoomAudioTranscript.title: Server Room Audio Transcript
serverRoomAudioTranscript.content: Recovered acoustic transcript: three knocks, one-second pause, three knocks, then a low-frequency reply from behind the server room wall. The repeated pattern is annotated as KNOCK-3 by the local indexer.

eastArchiveAccessMap.title: East Archive Access Map
eastArchiveAccessMap.content: Recovered map fragment shows an east archive maintenance passage that does not appear in the public facility plan. Two access vectors converge on the archive door at 22:58:04.

relayFailureReport.title: Relay Failure Report
relayFailureReport.content: Uplink core analysis: external communication failed in three pulses. The final pulse originated inside the facility, not from the relay tower. Recommended action: trace the blackout source through internal routing node H-0.

messages.first: If the relay goes dark, do not look outside. The signal source is still inside Helix.
messages.second: Then the blackout was not a failure. It was containment.

recovery.title: Recovery Lab
recovery.subtitle: Work against a damaged system image. Scan, repair, and rebuild partial records without touching the original archive.

resultStatuses.missing-repair: Repair output required before deep recovery.

relayCache.title: Relay Cache Index
relayCache.content: Relay cache index recovered from a damaged system copy.
relayCache.damagedContent:
The server r### experienced unau###ized access during maintenance window.
Power relay route H-0 was moved to int###al control.
Fragment references: file_12 / file_18 / file_21.
Checksum: PARTIAL // source image unstable.

maintenanceReport.title: Power Relay Maintenance Report
maintenanceReport.content:
Power Relay Maintenance Report

Maintenance window opened at 21:40 under relay route H-0.
The external relay was not failing before the blackout. It was redirected through an internal maintenance path.
Manual override authorization was recorded from inside Helix Labs.
The blackout sequence matches a deliberate reroute, not a tower outage.

file12.title: Recovery Fragment 12
file12.content: Line 01: Maintenance window opened under relay route H-0.

file18.title: Recovery Fragment 18
file18.content: Line 02: External relay traffic was redirected through an internal maintenance path.

file21.title: Recovery Fragment 21
file21.content: Line 03: Manual override originated inside Helix Labs before the blackout.

assembly.title: Fragment assembly
assembly.hint: Order the fragments by line marker, then assemble the maintenance report.

evidence.title: Evidence Board
evidence.subtitle: Unlocked evidence appears here as the investigation advances.
evidence.selectHint: Open an evidence card to inspect the full investigation file.
evidence.lockedHint: Read files, chats, media, and important logs to surface links.

serverRoomKnockPattern.title: Server Room Knock Pattern
serverRoomKnockPattern.description: A recovered note suggests repeated knocks near the server room may be a signal or response pattern.
serverRoomKnockPattern.longDescription: The recovered note describes three knocks from the server room and a single reply from the wall. The phrase is too specific to treat as incidental graffiti and may indicate a physical signaling pattern inside a sealed area.
serverRoomKnockPattern.forensicNotes: No audio file has been recovered, but the wording matches the timing gap before camera static on sublevel C. Marked for cross-check against future acoustic or maintenance records.

eastArchiveBreach.title: East Archive Breach
eastArchiveBreach.description: The archive door reports an impossible access event: opened from both sides within the same second.
eastArchiveBreach.longDescription: The east archive door log contains contradictory direction vectors. If the data is accurate, the door registered two opposing access events at the same timestamp, suggesting either log tampering or a duplicated physical state.
eastArchiveBreach.forensicNotes: The incident aligns with biometric verification warnings and a still image showing the door simultaneously open and sealed. This is the strongest facility breach indicator currently available.

duplicateCredential.title: Duplicate Credential
duplicateCredential.description: Credential N-KADE-17 authenticated inside Helix Labs while its owner claimed to be offsite.
duplicateCredential.longDescription: Authentication records place credential N-KADE-17 at terminal C-17 while Noah Kade reported being away from the facility. The conflict ties the external email report to the internal auth gateway event.
duplicateCredential.forensicNotes: The terminal was previously marked offline, increasing the likelihood of credential replay, cloned identity, or a forged auth event.

relatedFiles.kadeEmail: Emails / Badge rejected at west service gate

loginAnomalyNKade17.title: Login anomaly N-KADE-17
loginAnomalyNKade17.description: The authentication log places N-KADE-17 at terminal C-17 while the owner was offsite.
loginAnomalyNKade17.longDescription: The login anomaly is the raw system event behind the duplicate credential: one credential appears both outside the facility and inside terminal C-17 during the blackout window.
loginAnomalyNKade17.forensicNotes: This is not just an account mismatch. It anchors the credential conflict to a specific terminal and time window.

encryptedFragment01.title: Encrypted Fragment 01
encryptedFragment01.description: The decrypted fragment confirms the signal source remained inside Helix after the relay failed.
encryptedFragment01.longDescription: Encrypted Fragment 01 reframes the outage as containment rather than a random failure. Its wording points back to the knock pattern and suggests someone expected the blackout before it happened.
encryptedFragment01.forensicNotes: The fragment became readable only after the KNOCK-3 phrase was provided. That makes the server room pattern a likely access phrase instead of a coincidental rhythm.

eastArchiveAccessMap.title: East Archive Access Map
eastArchiveAccessMap.description: A recovered map shows a concealed maintenance passage feeding into the east archive.
eastArchiveAccessMap.longDescription: The access map adds physical context to the east archive breach. Two hidden vectors converge on the same archive door, matching the contradictory door event.
eastArchiveAccessMap.forensicNotes: The map was absent from the public facility plan. Its recovery strengthens the possibility that the breach used internal routing or privileged knowledge.

relayFailureReport.title: Relay Failure Report
relayFailureReport.description: The final relay failure pulse originated inside the facility instead of the tower.
relayFailureReport.longDescription: The report places the last communication failure within Helix Labs. That shifts the blackout from external equipment failure toward deliberate internal action.
relayFailureReport.forensicNotes: The internal pulse aligns with the duplicate credential anomaly and the encrypted warning that the signal source was still inside Helix.

recoveredFragmentSet.title: Recovered Fragment
recoveredFragmentSet.description: Recovered relay fragments point toward a missing maintenance record.
recoveredFragmentSet.longDescription: The fragments are incomplete on their own, but their line markers and relay references indicate they belong to the same maintenance report.
recoveredFragmentSet.forensicNotes: Recovered from a damaged copy of the relay cache. The fragments retain enough sequence data to support reconstruction.

powerRelayMaintenanceReport.title: Power Relay Maintenance Report
powerRelayMaintenanceReport.description: The reconstructed maintenance report says the relay was redirected internally before the blackout.
powerRelayMaintenanceReport.longDescription: The recovered report ties the blackout to a maintenance route change. The external relay did not simply fail; traffic was rerouted through an internal path.
powerRelayMaintenanceReport.forensicNotes: Integrity is high enough to treat the report as corroborating evidence, but the original system image remains damaged.

connections.title: Connection Board
connections.justificationQuestion: Why do you think these pieces of evidence are related?

justifications.sharedClue: They match by time, place, or code.
justifications.sameFileType: They are the same file type.

reasons.accessPhrase: KNOCK-3 was an access phrase hidden in the recovered note.
reasons.duplicateLogin: The same credential appears in an impossible location.
reasons.archiveRoute: Both clues indicate an alternate physical access route.
reasons.maintenanceRelay: The relay failure matches manipulation during maintenance.

deductions.title: Deductions
deductions.lockedDescription: Create the right evidence connection to resolve this deduction.

accessPhrase.title: The knock pattern was used as an access phrase
accessPhrase.description: The KNOCK-3 rhythm is not just a clue; it is the phrase that opens Encrypted Fragment 01.

internalInvolvement.title: The breach required internal access
internalInvolvement.description: The east archive breach and hidden access map point to a physical route that required internal knowledge.

maintenanceReroute.title: The blackout followed a maintenance reroute
maintenanceReroute.description: The relay failure report and reconstructed maintenance record show the outage was routed from inside Helix.

chapterSummary.title: Chapter 1 Summary

points.unauthorized: Unauthorized access occurred inside the facility.
points.serverRoom: The server room is connected to the recovered phrase.
points.internal: Someone with internal access may be involved.

chapterSummary.hook: The sequence is clearer, but the source inside Helix has not been identified. Chapter 2 remains open.

messages.CAM-500: Unauthorized access detected. Camera packet integrity failed.

messages.OCC-204: Sublevel C occupancy changed from 0 to 44 without door movement.

messages.CON-101: Verified evidence connection saved to local board.
messages.CON-422: Connection rejected: relationship was not sufficiently justified.

dataTexts.Helix Labs lost all communication with i: Helix Labs lost all communication with its employees after a staged maintenance window. The remaining system image contains emails, chats, files, media captures, and logs that suggest the blackout was deliberate.
dataTexts.Elias,\n\nThe board wants a clean incide:
Elias,

The board wants a clean incident summary before the morning call. Keep the language operational. Do not mention the east archive breach until we know whether the door logs are corrupt or altered.

If anyone asks about the silence from sublevel C, route it through me.

Mara
dataTexts.Mara,\n\nI can make the report look rout:
Mara,

I can make the report look routine, but the access pattern is not routine. Someone authenticated as Kade from a terminal that has been offline since July.

Security cameras show static at the exact same minute.

Elias
dataTexts.Scheduled maintenance will begin at 23:0: Scheduled maintenance will begin at 23:00 and may interrupt external communication. Local systems should remain available. Report unusual device behavior to Security Operations.
dataTexts.Badge rejected at west service gate: Badge rejected at west service gate
dataTexts.My badge failed twice at west service. T:
My badge failed twice at west service. The console says I am already inside the facility. I am not. I left at 18:12 and am writing this from home.

Please check for a duplicate credential.
dataTexts.At 23:17 local facility time, Helix Labs:
At 23:17 local facility time, Helix Labs stopped responding to all external communication attempts. Network telemetry shows a gradual degradation rather than a single outage. Internal authentication remained active for nine minutes after the external blackout.

Open questions: why did credential N-KADE-17 authenticate from an offline terminal, and why were occupancy sensors overwritten in sublevel C?
dataTexts.The east archive door should remain seal: The east archive door should remain sealed until biometric records can be manually verified. If the door log is accurate, someone opened it from both sides within the same second.
dataTexts.Night shift roster lists 43 employees on: Night shift roster lists 43 employees onsite. Badge telemetry lists 44. One unknown credential appears only as SIGNAL-000 and has no matching personnel record.
dataTexts.Three knocks from the server room. One r: Three knocks from the server room. One reply from the wall. The lights know before we do.
dataTexts.Last intact frame before static consumed: Last intact frame before static consumed the feed.
dataTexts.Door sensor reports open and sealed stat: Door sensor reports open and sealed states simultaneously.
dataTexts.External links drop in a spiral pattern: External links drop in a spiral pattern around core node H-0.
dataTexts.Anyone else seeing the internal map flic: Anyone else seeing the internal map flicker between floors?
dataTexts.Yes. Sublevel C keeps showing as unoccup: Yes. Sublevel C keeps showing as unoccupied, then full occupancy, then offline.
dataTexts.Do not go down there. The elevator just: Do not go down there. The elevator just accepted a call without anyone pressing it.
[encrypted fragment] HELIX IS NOT A LAB. IT IS A LOCK.
dataTexts.Who are you? How did you get into this c: Who are you? How did you get into this channel?
dataTexts.Emails / Badge rejected at west service: Emails / Badge rejected at west service gate

help.title: How to Play
help.subtitle: DeadSignal is a digital forensic investigation.

intro.copyOne: You received a recovered copy of the Helix Labs system, a company that lost all communication during a maintenance window.
intro.copyTwo: Your objective is to reconstruct what happened.
intro.copyThree: The case will not solve itself just because you read everything. You need to connect the right information.

steps.1: Read documents, emails, chats, system logs, and multimedia files.
steps.3: When you discover a clue, it is added to the Evidence Board.
steps.4: Use the Evidence Board to connect related clues.
steps.6: To advance, you will need to find passwords, unlock files, recover fragments, and prove deductions.
steps.7: The objectives on the right show which investigation lines are still open.
steps.8: Look for relationships by time, place, person, code, or event.

currentObjective.title: Current objective
currentObjective.body: Discover what caused the Helix Labs blackout and who may have been involved.

tip.title: Tip
tip.body: Do not connect clues at random. Read the content and look for shared times, locations, people, codes, or events.

reset.confirm: This will restart the investigation, clear progress, evidence, connections, notes, recovery state, deductions, and dynamic logs. The selected language will be kept.
